metadata.seed: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

patient.query: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

llm_response.response:
It sounds like you're in a really tough spot, wrestling with a deep and genuine love. It's completely valid to feel that way when someone holds such a significant place in your heart, and it's brave of you to acknowledge the pain of living without them. Sometimes, the depth of a connection makes the prospect of moving on feel impossible, and it's okay to grieve that loss even if the relationship isn't what it once was – your feelings are real and deserve to be honored.



Perhaps exploring *why* she feels so special might offer some clarity, and whether there's a way to navigate the situation with honesty and self-compassion.
